title: The ethics of space tourism
explanation:
The ethics of space tourism

Commercial space flights for wealthy tourists have moved from science fiction to reality. Supporters claim the industry will inspire innovation and fund research that benefits everyone. Critics respond that the enormous cost of a single ticket could finance thousands of clean water projects on Earth. They also question the environmental impact of rocket launches. As space tourism expands, society will need clear rules about safety, access and the responsible use of resources both on Earth and beyond.

questions.text: Space tourism is now a [*commercial reality|impossible dream|school subject only].
Supporters say the industry may fund research that benefits [*everyone|only pilots|only artists].
Critics compare ticket prices with funding for clean [*water projects|football stadiums|bookshops].
Society may need rules about safety, access and responsible use of [*resources|jokes|music].

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: Critics also question the environmental impact of rocket ____.  | *launches | books | meals
Early tourists are mostly very ____.  | *wealthy | young | silent
Supporters believe the industry can inspire ____.  | *innovation | silence | hunger
The debate is partly ____.  | *ethical | musical | culinary
The article presents [*arguments on both sides|no viewpoints|only jokes].
The writer suggests future regulation will be [*necessary|impossible|irrelevant].
The issue connects technology with questions of [*justice and environment|fashion and sport|food and travel only].
